page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AWS Graviton processor. It features a single dedicated physical core (1 vCPU) based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture (Neoverse-V1) running at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1516.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1430.0. However, multi-core performance is limited by the single-core configuration, resulting in lower scores across multi-threaded benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is measured at 96.84 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 29.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded compression tasks perform moderately well, with gzip compression reaching 23.74 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.2 GB/sec. Database and web serving metrics are lower, with Redis SET operations achieving 1.71 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 1.89 GB/sec.

Given its single-core ARM architecture and integrated 59 GB NVMe SSD local storage, the m7gd.medium offers a resource-dense, entry-level option for workloads requiring local scratch space without high CPU concurrency. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides a qualitative cost advantage for applications that would otherwise require external block storage. This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations that benefit from local SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.medium is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 core running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory, 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers average single-core performance, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 1430.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1516.99. Due to its single-core configuration, multi-threaded performance and memory bandwidth are limited. The integrated NVMe SSD provides efficient local storage for applications requiring fast scratch space. This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded tasks, development environments, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring a 2.6 GHz Graviton processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: An entry-level ARM64 instance featuring dedicated Graviton compute and integrated NVMe SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor

Suitable for development environments, basic web serving, and small databases
